A lock-free ring buffer manages wraparound by treating the underlying array as a circular address space, where indices are incremented modulo the buffer size to seamlessly loop from the end back to the beginning without explicit conditional branching. Memory ordering is critical to prevent data races between concurrent producers and consumers, requiring atomic operations with specific acquire and release semantics to ensure that writes to buffer slots are visible to readers only after the head pointer update is committed, thereby preventing the consumption of uninitialized or stale data.
